item.title: Don’t mistake friction for a broken alliance with Israel
item.description: Presidential spats with Israeli prime ministers are nothing new.

item.title: The NRA is almost out of bullets
item.description: The legal battle facing the gun rights organization is an existential threat.

item.title: This common women’s health issue is getting a new name. It’s about time.
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome was always a misnomer. Its new name will help women get proper treatment.

item.title: JD Vance, Iran deal architect and scapegoat
item.description: The vice president is tasked with helping the regime “learn the ways of peace.” Good luck.

item.title: How the Trump administration built its exit ramp in Iran
item.description: Kushner, Witkoff and a Qatari mediator turned an investor pitch into a fragile peace deal.

item.title: The nest egg
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Social Security solvency.

item.title: Why Gen Z hates capitalism
item.description: Has Gen Z completely given up on capitalism — and America? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," James Hohmann sits down with columnist and “Reasonably Optimistic” host Megan McArdle to explore why young voters are turning to socialism and how politicians like Zohran Mamdani are capitalizing on their angst.

item.title: Switzerland says ‘no’ to anti-immigration insanity
item.description: Voters reject a cap on the nation’s population.

item.title: Putin calls Zelensky illegitimate. He’s describing himself.
item.description: The Russian dictator’s obsession reveals exactly what occupies his own subconscious.

item.title: Here’s the real Democratic autopsy
item.description: Why did the party lose in ’24? Neither Jill Biden’s memoir nor the DNC’s postmortem has the answer.

item.title: Culture wars aren’t the only problem with two new Smithsonian museums
item.description: The Women's History and Latino Museums should be built on the Energy Department headquarters site.

item.title: The marriage incentive no one talks about
item.description: Economics may no longer be an incentive to marry, but feeling secure is.
